channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I built a browser-only SQL practice tool because installing DBeaver is a productivity tax
item.description: Recently, I was tutoring data analysts through Preply when I noticed something weird. Every single student — regardless of whether they were prepping for an Amazon interview, a Snowflake analyst...

item.title: react-atom-trigger v2: A Scroll Trigger Recipe
item.description: Some react-waypoint instinct, a bit of IntersectionObserver magic, a lot of geometry and just enough scheduler spice to keep it sane. I did not want to write just another "how to use my library"...

item.title: 🚀 Weekly Progress: Building My MERN + Microservices Pizza App (Learning in Public)
item.description: This week was all about connecting the system, enforcing code quality, and pushing myself to build like a real engineer — not a tutorial follower. 🔧 What I accomplished this week • Integrated...

item.title: The Page Order Math Behind Saddle-Stitch Booklets Is Weirder Than You Think. So I Automated It. [Devlog #8]
item.description: All tests run on an 8-year-old MacBook Air. Print pages 1, 2, 3, 4 in order, fold the paper — and the result is wrong. Booklet printing requires a completely different page ordering. For an 8-page...

item.title: Moon
item.description: Check out this Pen I made!

item.title: I Built a Multi-Step AI Agent in One Day with Google ADK — Here's What Nobody Tells You
item.description: I'm a final-year computer science student. I spend most of my days training deep learning models on image datasets, debugging tensor shape errors at 2am, and convincing myself that 67% accuracy is...

item.title: The Credential Vending Revolution: How Polaris Eliminates Long-Lived Keys
item.description: Author: Prithvi S Date: April 23, 2026 Topic: Apache Polaris, Credential Vending, Security Architecture Word Count: ~2,100 words The Problem Nobody Wants to Talk About You're a data engineer at a...

item.title: The Hidden Side of Being a Developer: Code, Pressure, and Health
item.description: When people think about developers, they usually imagine someone sitting in front of a laptop, typing fast, building cool apps, and earning well. That’s true… but it’s not the full story. Behind...

item.title: How to Build Accessible Custom Dropdowns (Comboboxes) That Actually Work
item.description: Originally published on the AccessGuard blog. After our post on accessible modals resonated with so many of you, we wanted to tackle another notoriously tricky pattern: the custom dropdown, or...

item.title: Launching S3 Files, making S3 buckets accessible as file systems
item.description: 🚀 Amazon S3 Just Got More Interesting: AWS Launches S3 Files AWS just introduced S3 Files, and this could quietly become one of the most useful storage launches for modern cloud workloads. 👀 For...

item.title: China-Linked GopherWhisper Infects 12 Mongolian Government Systems with Go Backdoors
item.description: Mongolian governmental institutions have emerged as the target of a previously undocumented China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group tracked as GopherWhisper. "The group wields a wide...

item.title: Elevating Austria: Google invests in its first data center in the Alps.
item.description: Aerial image of the data center

item.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
item.description: Vercel on Wednesday revealed that it has identified an additional set of customer accounts that were compromised as part of a security incident that enabled unauthorized access to its internal...

item.title: Apple Patches iOS Flaw That Stored Deleted Signal Notifications in FBI Forensic Case
item.description: Apple has rolled out a software fix for iOS and iPadOS to address a Notification Services flaw that stored notifications marked for deletion on the device. The vulnerability, tracked as...

item.title: Honor’s new phones look like iPhones for Android
item.description: Honor has announced the 600 and 600 Pro, which it calls "accessible flagships," and they look… familiar. Especially in that orange. The Pro makes the iPhone comparison especially obvious thanks to...

item.title: India’s app market is booming — but global platforms are capturing most of the gains
item.description: Non-gaming apps, led by streaming and AI, are driving growth, even as India's spending per user lags global peers.

item.title: The Iranian women Trump ‘saved’ from execution are simultaneously real and AI-manipulated
item.description: On Wednesday, President Donald Trump claimed to have secured the release of eight Iranian women condemned to execution for protesting the regime. Only the night before, he had posted on Truth...

item.title: Tesla just increased its spending plan to $25B — here’s where the money is going
item.description: Tesla's planned capex for 2026 is three times higher than what the company has historically spent. Its CFO said, as a result, Tesla will have a negative free cash flow the rest of the year.

item.title: Google updates Workspace to make AI your new office intern
item.description: Google has introduced a host of new automated functions into Workspace, all of which are driven by Workspace Intelligence, its new AI system.

item.title: Elon Musk admits that millions of Tesla vehicles won’t get unsupervised FSD
item.description: Tesla vehicles with the company's Hardware 3 (HW3) computer actually won't receive unsupervised Full Self-Driving (FSD), CEO Elon Musk said on Wednesday's Q1 2026 earnings call. Approximately 4...

item.title: Hands on with X’s new AI-powered custom feeds
item.description: X's AI-powered custom timelines are replacing Communities, with Grok-curated feeds...and new ad slots.

item.title: Sam Altman’s Orb Company Promoted a Bruno Mars Partnership That Doesn't Exist
item.description: “To be clear, we were never approached … nor were we in any discussions regarding a partnership or tour access,” a spokesperson for the artist tells WIRED.

item.title: X is going to let Grok curate your timeline
item.description: X is putting its AI chatbot, Grok, in charge of your timeline. In an announcement on Wednesday, X product head Nikita Bier says Premium subscribers on iOS can get early access to a feature that...

item.title: Tesla’s revenue rises again as it prepares for more AI and robotics
item.description: Tesla released its 2026 first-quarter financial earnings today, providing another look at the progress of Elon Musk's $1 trillion bet to transform his company into a leader of AI and robotics....

item.title: AI failure could trigger the next financial crisis, warns Elizabeth Warren
item.description: "I know a bubble when I see one." That's what Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-MA), who led the push to create a new consumer financial regulator in the wake of the 2008 recession, told a crowd at a...

item.title: OpenAI now lets teams make custom bots that can do work on their own
item.description: OpenAI is giving users of its Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans access to cloud-based "workspace" agents available in ChatGPT that can perform business tasks. In its blog post, OpenAI...

item.title: Alexis Ohanian shocks Washington with pro-immigration remarks
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge readers about tech politics, political tech, and how they're muddying the waters of Washington, DC. My birthday is this week, and if you're...

item.title: How SpaceX preempted a $2B fundraise with a $60B buyout offer
item.description: Cursor was on track to close a $2 billion funding round this week but chose to halt discussions after SpaceX offered a $10 billion "collaboration fee" and a path to a $60 billion acquisition.

item.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
item.description: When authentication fails, things can go very, very wrong.

item.title: Former MrBeast exec sues over ‘years’ of alleged harassment
item.description: A former employee of Jimmy "MrBeast" Donaldson has filed a lawsuit alleging that she faced "intentional infliction of emotional distress" from harassment at the YouTuber's production company, was...
